reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to plan remediate: ensure aide installed, and /etc/aide.conf contains lines for auditctl, auditd, ausearch. Use sed to modify or add. Must not duplicate. Must handle commented lines.

Plan description: Use run_cmd with dnf install -y aide presuming not installed. Use read_file to read /etc/aide.conf. Check if lines exist for each tool. For each file: /usr/sbin/auditctl, /usr/sbin/auditd, /usr/sbin/ausearch. For each, use sed -i to substitute if line exists (including commented). use regex to match leading '#?' then spaces then path. Replacement: path p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512. If doesn't exist, append with echo >>.

Thus minimal changes. We'll also ensure pattern anchored start. Use sed -i -r "s|^(\\s*#?\\s*/usr/sbin/auditctl\\b).*|\\1 p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512|". Wait we need to preserve preceding indent. Use \1. But we need capture path part. So: s|^(\\s*#?\\s*/usr/sbin/auditctl\\b).*|\\1 p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512|.

If line not found: echo "/usr/sbin/auditctl p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512" >> /etc/aide.conf.

Do similarly for auditd and ausearch.

Check with grep -P '^\s*#?\s*/usr/sbin/auditctl\b' /etc/aide.conf to detect. The regex includes word boundary.

We'll produce plan description accordingly.

Now call review_plan.
